items.final_wording: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management
source.population: People aged 16 years and over with suspected sepsis, excluding people who are or have recently been pregnant.
source.setting: Community, ambulance, acute hospital, and emergency assessment settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for suspected sepsis recognition and assessment in non-pregnant people aged 16 years and over; partial for a broad fever or suspected-infection workflow and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendation 1.3 — history, observations, circulation, respiration, skin, urine output, and source examination

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

items.rationale: Supports entered acute observations, circulation, respiration, skin, urine and source examination.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting observed changes and, when performed by a competent practitioner, the named structured assessment and clinician-confirmed diagnosis; no diagnosis is inferred by the template.

source.title: Delirium: prevention, diagnosis and management in hospital and long-term care
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE legal and pathway adaptation
source.population: People aged 18 years and over in hospital or long-term residential or nursing care.
source.setting: Hospital and long-term care; the acute-hospital recommendations are partially applicable to emergency assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact only for recent fluctuating cognitive, perceptual, physical-function and social-behaviour changes, documentation of those changes, and use of a competent structured assessment. It does not establish delirium or cover every cause of altered mental status.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.5.2–1.6.4 — document changes and competent assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting observed changes and, when performed by a competent practitioner, the named structured assessment and clinician-confirmed diagnosis; no diagnosis is inferred by the template.

items.rationale: Supports daily change documentation and competent ICU delirium assessment.

items.final_wording: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE medicines, prescribing, pharmacy, discharge, and information-governance adaptation
source.population: Adults, children, and young people using medicines, with specific transfer, reconciliation, and decision-involvement recommendations.
source.setting: Transfers between care settings and clinician-led medicines consultations.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting current medicines, changes made during transfer, reconciliation, discrepancies, person or carer involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-led decisions. It does not generate a medicine start, stop, dose, prescription, or deprescribing decision.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.
